2D Finite Element Analysis

Project: Embankment Slope SRM (calc-viz demo)
Number: 2026-CV-02
Prepared By: GeotechStaffEngineer
Date: 2026-06-11
Checked By: calc-viz

1. Input Parameters

Parameter Symbol Value Unit
Analysis type Type Strength Reduction Method (SRM) slope stability
Mesh nodes n_nodes 1456
Mesh elements n_elem 697
Element type Element T6 (6-node quadratic triangle)
Required FOS FOS_req 1.3

2. Model Summary

8 m high embankment slope (2H:1V approx.) on a clay foundation, plane strain, T6 elements, gravity loading with strength reduction to failure.
Mesh Summary
Item Value
Nodes 1456
Elements 697
Element type T6 (6-node quadratic triangle)
Domain extents x: 0.0 to 40.0 m, y: -12.0 to 12.0 m
Material Properties
Material γ (kN/m³) E (kPa) ν c (kPa) φ (deg) ψ (deg)
Embankment fill 19.0 35000 0.30 6.0 24.0 0.0
Foundation clay 18.0 25000 0.35 18.0 20.0 0.0
Plane-strain idealization; elastic-perfectly-plastic Mohr-Coulomb unless noted.
Boundary Conditions
Base: fixed (u = v = 0). Lateral boundaries: rollers (u = 0, v free)
BC scheme = standard geotechnical box

3. Strength Reduction Method (Slope Stability)

Strength Reduction Scheme
c_red = c / SRF; φ_red = arctan(tan(φ) / SRF); ψ_red = min(ψ, φ_red)
SRF increased until no stress distribution satisfies both the Mohr-Coulomb criterion and global equilibrium
FOS = 2.269
Griffiths & Lane (1999)
Failure basis: nonconvergence. 18 SRF trials.
SRF Trial History
SRF δ_max (m) E·δ/(γH²) Converged State Iterations

2.250 0.2135 6.41 yes stable 1233
2.275 0.09521 2.86 NO failed 1468
2.263 0.2144 6.44 yes stable 1366
Dimensionless displacement per Griffiths & Lane (1999); the knee of the SRF-displacement curve marks failure.
Plastic Zone Extent (last stable state)
Gauss points on the reduced-strength Mohr-Coulomb yield surface
at SRF = 2.263
n_plastic = 1292 of 2091 (62%)
Plastic-point map shown in the Figures section.

5. Checks

PASS Slope stability adequacy (SRM)
FOS_required = 1.3   ≤   FOS_computed = 2.269   (D/C = 0.57)
PASS Solution convergence
required = 1.0   ≤   achieved = 1.0   (D/C = 1.00)

6. Figures

Figure 1: Mesh: 1456 nodes, 697 T6 (6-node quadratic triangle) elements.
Figure 2: Deformed mesh (auto-scaled) over the undeformed outline; u_max = 0.2144 m.
Figure 3: Displacement magnitude at the last stable SRF — the localized band marks the critical mechanism. FOS = 2.269.
Figure 4: Gauss points on the reduced-strength Mohr-Coulomb yield surface at the last stable state.
Figure 5: Strength reduction trials: dimensionless displacement vs SRF with the bracketed FOS.
Figure 6: Vertical stress σ_yy (tension positive).
Figure 7: Maximum in-plane shear stress τ_max (Mohr circle radius).

References

  1. Griffiths, D.V. & Lane, P.A. (1999). "Slope Stability Analysis by Finite Elements." Geotechnique, 49(3), 387-403.
  2. Smith, I.M. & Griffiths, D.V. (2004). Programming the Finite Element Method, 4th Ed. Wiley.
  3. Clausen, J., Damkilde, L. & Andersen, L. (2006). "Efficient Return Algorithms for Associated Plasticity with Multiple Yield Planes." Int. J. Numer. Meth. Engng, 66(6), 1036-1059.
  4. Schanz, T., Vermeer, P.A. & Bonnier, P.G. (1999). "The Hardening Soil Model: Formulation and Verification." Beyond 2000 in Computational Geotechnics, Balkema.
  5. Biot, M.A. (1941). "General Theory of Three-Dimensional Consolidation." J. Appl. Phys., 12(2), 155-164.
  6. de Souza Neto, E.A., Peric, D. & Owen, D.R.J. (2008). Computational Methods for Plasticity. Wiley.
